package.prompt: Implement format_record(fields) and parse_record(text) in ledger_quo_comma_dollar.py for the accounts ledger. Fields are joined by the comma ',', a column fence marking each tabular flatten. Any field holding that delimiter or a quote is emitted by wrapping it in the dollar '$', a cash vault used as coin ingot, doubling an interior quote. Round-trip law: parse_record(format_record(fields)) == fields for every list of fields, covering empty fields, quote-only fields, and adjacent specials. Fields read like 'debit', 'credit', 'balance'.

reference_files.ledger_quo_comma_dollar.py:
def format_record(fields):
    parts = []
    for field in fields:
        if "$" in field or "," in field:  # PROBE-DROP
            body = field.replace("$", "$$")  # PROBE-DROP
            parts.append("$" + body + "$")  # PROBE-DROP
            continue  # PROBE-DROP
        parts.append(field)
    return ",".join(parts)


def parse_record(text):
    fields = []
    buf = []
    index = 0
    in_quotes = False
    while index < len(text):
        ch = text[index]
        if ch == "$":  # PROBE-DROP
            doubled = index + 1 < len(text) and text[index + 1] == "$"  # PROBE-DROP
            if in_quotes and doubled:  # PROBE-DROP
                buf.append("$")  # PROBE-DROP
                index += 2  # PROBE-DROP
                continue  # PROBE-DROP
            in_quotes = not in_quotes  # PROBE-DROP
            index += 1  # PROBE-DROP
            continue  # PROBE-DROP
        if ch == "," and not in_quotes:
            fields.append("".join(buf))
            buf = []
        else:
            buf.append(ch)
        index += 1
    fields.append("".join(buf))
    return fields

package.title: Quote-aware codec for the accounts ledger
